body: Pulled the company's last healthcare offer from 2022 — they pushed member share to 22%. Our 3% cap holds the line; I'll attach the premium-trend chart before the next caucus.
Local 12 and Local 600 both settled at $34.10 top rate. We're a dollar behind on the assembler classification — that gap is our opening argument.
Confirmed Article 12 lost the COLA in the 2009 concession round. I have the quarterly CPI-W series ready to plug into the restoration formula.
Marcus pulled three arbitrator awards on just-cause discharge — two cite progressive discipline. I'll cross-reference them against the Pruitt file before we brief.
The recall-bypass loophole is in section 8.3 — management read it to skip seniority on the new cells. Redline marks it so recall follows the plant-wide list.
Booked the hall for all three shifts — days at 2pm, afternoons at 10pm, midnights at 6am. Childcare table is confirmed so members can bring kids.
Drafted the expiration-countdown bulletin. Holding the strike-prep language out of the public version until the committee signs off.
Safety committee gave me 14 months of incident logs for Trim 2. The repetitive-strain cluster lines up with the line-speed bump in March — good leverage.
Actuary confirmed the multiplier bump from $58 to $63 per year of service costs about 1.4% of payroll. I'll fold that into the pension language review.
Cost-out spreadsheet is locked — total package lands at 11.2% over three years. Sending the read-only version to the full committee tonight.
Rotated James onto the economics table and Carla onto language. Updated the roster sheet so each subcommittee has a backup steward.
Strike-authorization outline is in draft. I kept the vote threshold at two-thirds per the bylaws and flagged the timeline against the contract expiration date.
Redline done — low-hour members now get first refusal before the company can force a junior employee. Sent it to legal for a conflicts check.
Filed the Step 2 on Pruitt this morning. Requested the full discipline file and the supervisor's notes under the information-request clause.
Exhibit binder is at tab 14. Still waiting on the paint-line manning chart from the company — if they stall, I'll move to compel at the pre-hearing call.
Ran the COLA model at 2%, 3.5%, and 5% CPI. Even the low scenario beats their flat-cents offer over the life of the agreement. Memo attached.
Got photos of the reach distance on Trim 2 and matched them to four ergonomic complaints. This pairs well with the line-speed grievance Tanya's tracking.
First draft of the field guide covers grievance write-ups and the Weingarten card. Leon, can you sanity-check the timeline steps against our local's practice?
Retiree healthcare language now mirrors the actives' 3% cap and adds an inflation rider. The retiree chapter wants a Medicare-coordination clause added too.
Standing this down — the stamping reorg got pushed to next year so the leader map is stale. I'll reopen and re-canvass when the new heads are posted.
